phase_name: Refactor cmux-notify to class-based with event dispatch map

decisions.decision: Class-based with event dispatch map — no FSM library
decisions.rationale: typescript-fsm would add dependency overhead; pending-input tracking uses Sets (not single state), making a strict FSM awkward. Named-method dispatch map gives 90% of the readability win with zero new abstractions.
cmux-notify has no existing tests; refactor is behavior-preserving so tests lock in the contract before touching implementation

next_action: Open src/cmux-notify.test.ts and write the 10 behavior tests for the dispatch map routing and state management, then run vitest to confirm red. Then implement CmuxNotifyPlugin class.
context_notes: Phase 7 plan (07-01-PLAN.md) was just created by gsd-planner. Nothing has been implemented yet. The refactor converts cmux-notify.ts from closure-based (CmuxPlugin as a plain async function) to class-based (class CmuxNotifyPlugin extends PluginBase), mirroring the pattern in cmux-subagent-viewer.ts. The if-chain event handler becomes a dispatch map: Record<EventType, (event) => Promise<void>>. All closure state becomes class instance fields. Public API (CmuxPlugin factory export) is unchanged.
